Title: CRISPR/Cas9-mediated editing of 1-aminocyclopropane-1-carboxylate oxidase1 enhances flower longevity

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes flower senescence and shortens flower longevity in petunia
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Final step of ethylene formation from ACC
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes ACC to ethylene in the terminal step of ethylene biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as the primary determinant among tested ACO homologs for floral ethylene production in this cultivar; broader rate-limiting status not fully established
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Transcript low at budding stages 1-2, increases at early blooming stages 3-4, and highest at fully blooming stages 5-7 in petals
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in flowers/petals during late flowering and senescence-associated stages; reduced transcript levels in edited lines with lower ethylene
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Flower developmental stage; days after flowering
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CRISPR/Cas9 editing of PhACO1 reduces ethylene production in petals and pistils and extends flower longevity; stronger effect in homozygotes than monoallelic mutants
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression profiling across flowering stages; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout/editing; targeted deep sequencing; qRT-PCR; ethylene quantification; T1 inheritance analysis; off-target assessment by Sanger sequencing
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Arabidopsis thaliana U6 promoter (sgRNA, constitutive); CaMV 35S promoter (SpCas9, constitutive)
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Targeted deep sequencing (Illumina MiniSeq); no public accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for genome editing to reduce floral ethylene and extend vase/flower longevity in petunia and potentially other ornamental crops; homozygous edits provide stronger benefit with no obvious floral or vegetative morphology penalty reported
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No significant changes in vegetative or floral morphology were detected in evaluated T1 lines; broader physiological tradeoffs were not assessed
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No off-target mutations were detected in the homologous genes PhACO3 and PhACO4 in edited lines; T1 progeny retained edited alleles and phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PhACO1 showed the highest transcript abundance among tested ACO homologs during flowering and correlated with elevated ethylene production. 2. CRISPR/Cas9 editing of PhACO1 significantly decreased ethylene production and prolonged flower longevity in T0 and T1 plants. 3. Homozygous mutants showed stronger ethylene reduction and longevity extension than monoallelic mutants, with no detected off-target edits in PhACO3/PhACO4.
